title: 10 best herbal essential oils for anxiety
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for anxiety are plant-derived extracts that have been used for centuries to alleviate feelings of worry and unease. These oils contain bioactive compounds that interact with the brain's chemistry to promote relaxation, reducing anxiety symptoms. The best herbal essential oils for anxiety include Lavandula angustifolia, Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Anthemis nobilis, Avena sativa, Santalum album, Ginkgo biloba, Hypericum perforatum, and Boswellia serrata. If left untreated, anxiety can severely impact daily life, leading to sleep disturbances, social withdrawal, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting mental and physical well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with anxiety because its calming and soothing properties can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The sweet, floral scent of lavender oil has been shown to slow down heart rate and lower blood pressure, calming the nervous system and quieting a racing mind. By reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting a sense of calm, English lavender essential oils can help individuals cope with everyday worries and improve their overall mental well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) effects: The calming compounds in Lavender oil, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation and decreasing stress levels.
Sedative properties: Lavender oil's sedative effects can help slow down brain activity, reducing feelings of overwhelm, nervousness, and agitation that often accompany anxiety.

Antidepressant properties: The antidepressant compounds in Lavender oil may help reduce symptoms of depression, which are closely linked to anxiety, by increasing the production of serotonin and other neurotransmitters that regulate mood.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, as they contain a high concentration of calming compounds like apigenin.
Leaves: Lavender leaves are used because they possess a unique composition of volatile compounds that can help soothe the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers at dawn when fragrant oil content is highest for maximum potency.
Steam distill the harvested lavender flowers using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c temperature for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the obtained essential oil from steam distillation process into dark glass containers immediately.
Blend 10% lavandula angustifolia essential oil with 90% carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio.
Allow the blended mixture to mature for at least 24 hours before use or further processing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, reducing feelings of stress and tension. Additionally, the sweet and citrusy aroma of lemon balm can help to calm the mind and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Regular use of lemon balm essential oil can also help to reduce symptoms of anxiety disorders over time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress, which is a known contributor to anxiety and related mood disorders.

Adaptogenic properties: The essential oil has adaptogenic compounds that help the body respond to stress and promote balance in the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting relaxation.

GABA-ergic effects: Lemon balm essential oil contains compounds that interact with GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate calmness and relaxation, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms by promoting a sense of calm and reducing nervous tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, a potent natural anxiolytic that helps to soothe anxiety and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or late afternoon to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels to around 10%.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using a still with a 1:1 leaf-to-water ratio and a 4-hour processing time.
Collect the resulting essential oil by filtering it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles, storing them in a cool place to preserve quality.
Use the freshly produced melissa officinalis essential oil immediately or store it for up to 6 months in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and sedative properties, which help to reduce feelings of worry and stress. The oil contains valerenic acid, a compound that interacts with the brain's neurotransmitters to promote relaxation and calmness. When inhaled, it can slow down the body's natural response to stressors, allowing individuals to feel more grounded and centered. Additionally, its sweet, earthy aroma can help to distract from racing thoughts and promote a sense of tranquility, making it an effective natural remedy for anxiety relief.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (Anxiety-reducing) Properties: Valerian oil has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, which help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress.
Sedative Properties: The sedative properties of valerian oil can help to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce restlessness, which are all common symptoms of anxiety.

Muscle Relaxant Properties: Valerian oil's muscle relaxant properties can help to relieve physical tension and stiffness associated with anxiety, promoting a sense of calm and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help alleviate anxiety.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the sedative properties that can be used to treat anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh valeriana officinalis roots for optimal potency and aroma.
Cleanse the harvested roots by rinsing them thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm and well-ventilated area at 60°f to 70°f for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried valeriana officinalis roots using 2% of the root material and 98% of distilled water for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop essential oils helps with anxiety because of its unique ability to calm the mind and body. The oil's potent combination of sedative and analgesic properties works to reduce feelings of worry, fear, and apprehension. It also helps to regulate the nervous system, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility. Additionally, maypop essential oils' calming aroma can help to quiet the mind, making it easier to focus and sleep. By using maypop essential oils in aromatherapy or topical application, individuals can experience a natural and effective reduction in anxiety symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming: Maypop essential oil has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which helps to calm anxiety and promote relaxation.

Anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory: The anxiolytic properties of Maypop essential oil help to reduce feelings of anxiety and fear, while its anti-inflammatory effects can alleviate physical symptoms associated with anxiety such as headaches and muscle tension.

Adaptogenic and stress-relieving: Maypop essential oil is an adaptogen that helps the body cope with stress and promotes a sense of balance and well-being, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maypop roots have a calming effect and are often used in essential oils to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop plant contain a high concentration of fatty acids, which help to soothe nervous tension and calm the mind.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a warm air circulation method at 30-40°c for 24 hours.
Place the dried plant material into a stainless steel distillation apparatus with 200ml of distilled water and steam-distill for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil as it separates from the distillate and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.
Use the essential oil immediately or mix it with a carrier oil like jojoba at a ratio of 1:50 for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with anxiety because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Its calming scent can slow down heart rate and blood pressure, while also lowering cortisol levels. Additionally, chamomile oil's natural soothing properties help quiet the mind, allowing individuals to let go of worries and feel more centered, making it an effective natural remedy for managing anxiety and promoting a sense of calmness.
remedy_properties: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound that binds to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety symptoms by slowing down the brain's activity.
Chamazulene: A sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stress-induced inflammation in the body, contributing to feelings of anxiety and calmness.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene compound with anti-spasmodic and anti-anxiety effects, helping to soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an anti-anxiety compound that interacts with GABA receptors in the brain.
Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant have been traditionally used to calm nervousness and promote relaxation due to their rich content of calcium and magnesium.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers on a sunny morning after the dew has evaporated using scissors.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm ventilated area away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days or until completely dry using wire racks.
Combine 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar with lid.
Leave the mixture to steep for 2 weeks at room temperature shaking the jar daily to infuse the oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean container discarding the solids and store the infused oil in dark glass bottles for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with anxiety because it contains compounds that have a calming and relaxing effect on the mind and body. The scent of bitter orange oil has been shown to reduce stress and promote feelings of calmness, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. Additionally, its ability to regulate mood and emotions can help individuals cope better with stressful situations, leading to a more balanced emotional state.
remedy_properties: Citral: Citral has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm anxiety and promote relaxation by reducing nervous activity.
Limonene: Limonene has anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties and can help to alleviate feelings of stress and anxiety by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.

Vitamin C: Vitamin C, present in bitter orange essential oil, is a natural antioxidant that can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to anxiety disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and limonoids, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, making them a popular choice for calming and soothing essential oil blends.
Seeds: Containing high amounts of sesquiterpenes and other compounds, bitter orange seeds are believed to help reduce anxiety and stress by promoting relaxation and calming the mind.
remedy_recipe: Harvest citrus x aurantium peels from fresh organic fruits in quantities of approximately 500 grams.
Dry the harvested peels using a food dehydrator set at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried peels in a stainless steel still with distilled water at a ratio of 1:5 for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a paper coffee filter into dark glass bottles.
Store the citrus x aurantium essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months prior to use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's citrusy aroma has a direct impact on the nervous system, reducing feelings of tension and stress. It also possesses natural mood-boosting compounds that help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. When inhaled or applied topically, lemongrass essential oils promote relaxation, ease muscle cramps and spasms, and calm the mind, making it an effective natural remedy for managing anxiety and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Citral: Citral, a key component of lemongrass essential oil, has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by inhibiting the production of stress hormones and promoting relaxation.
Geraniol: Geraniol, another major constituent of lemongrass essential oil, has been found to possess sedative properties, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms such as insomnia and restlessness.

Beta-caryophyllene: Beta-caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene present in lemongrass essential oil, has been shown to have anxiolytic effects by interacting with the brain's serotonin system, which helps regulate mood and emotional response.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are known for their calming and sedative effects, making them suitable for reducing anxiety symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat nervous disorders and anxiety due to the presence of citral, a natural antidepressant compound.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass stalks from a pesticide-free field or your own garden weighing approximately 2-3 pounds.
Wash the harvested lemongrass stalks thoroughly in cold running water and gently scrub them with a soft-bristled brush.
Dry the washed lemongrass stalks using a food dehydrator set at 135°f for 24 hours or by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Steam-distill the dried lemongrass stalks using a still or distillation apparatus with a yield of approximately 10-15% essential oil.
Store the extracted cymbopogon citratus essential oils in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep them refrigerated for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and focusing properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil has a soothing effect on the mind, reducing feelings of overwhelm and stress. Its ability to improve concentration and mental clarity also helps individuals stay focused and composed during anxious moments. Additionally, inhaling peppermint oil's refreshing aroma can stimulate a sense of calmness and tranquility, promoting relaxation and alleviating symptoms of anxiety.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety and calming effects: Peppermint essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation by decreasing cortisol levels.

Pain relief and tension reduction: Peppermint essential oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate headaches, migraines, and muscle tension caused by anxiety, reducing overall stress and promoting feelings of calmness.

Mood enhancement and mood stabilizer: The menthol content in peppermint essential oil has a uplifting effect on the mind and body, helping to improve mood, reduce irritability, and promote a sense of well-being, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which has been shown to have calming and soothing properties, making it effective in reducing anxiety.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves are rich in menthone, a compound that has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from organic farms or your garden ensuring at least 2 pounds of leaves for extraction.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a single layer on paper towels or a wire rack within 24 hours.
Combine dried peppermint leaves with carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil at a ratio of 1 part peppermint to 3 parts carrier oil.
Steam distill the peppermint and carrier oil mixture for approximately 2-3 hours extracting essential oils and releasing natural compounds.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil blend into dark glass bottles within an hour of extraction preserving freshness and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with anxiety because of its unique composition, which includes sesquiterpenes that have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds work to reduce feelings of stress and tension, promoting relaxation and serenity. Additionally, the oil's citrusy scent can help uplift and calm the mind, reducing racing thoughts and worries associated with anxiety. By inhaling Tasmanian blue gum essential oil, individuals can experience a sense of peacefulness and tranquility, making it an effective natural remedy for managing anxiety.
remedy_properties: Sesquiterpenoids: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil contains high levels of sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with the brain's neurotransmitters and promoting relaxation.

Borneol: Borneol is a constituent of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil that has been found to exhibit sedative and calming effects, making it useful for reducing anxiety and promoting feelings of calmness.

Citronellal: Citronellal, another component of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil, has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety such as restlessness and irritability.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, making them ideal for creating a calming and soothing essential oil.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress by promoting relaxation and tranquility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours.
Crush dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the powdered eucalyptus globulus in a 1:5 ratio with distilled water for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle essential oils helps with anxiety because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids and silymarin compounds. These potent bioactive agents work together to promote a sense of calmness and relaxation, soothing the mind and body. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce cortisol levels, further alleviating feelings of stress and worry. By incorporating milk thistle essential oils into one's daily routine, individuals can experience a natural and holistic approach to managing anxiety, promoting overall well-being and peace of mind.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety compounds: Milk Thistle Essential Oil contains a significant amount of flavonoids, lignans, and saponins, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, helping to calm the mind and body.
Adaptogenic properties: The oil has adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and anxiety by regulating cortisol levels, promoting relaxation, and improving mood stability.

Sedative and calming effects: Milk Thistle Essential Oil's sedative and calming effects are attributed to its ability to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, which helps regulate sleep, reduces anxiety and stress, and promotes feelings of calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic properties, making it effective in reducing anxiety symptoms.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds are rich in unsaturated fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress-related anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum seeds and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the cleaned seeds in a double boiler with 10g of seeds and 100ml of distilled water.
Collect the essential oil that is obtained after distillation and separate it from the floral water.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label them properly.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 2 weeks before using it for anxiety relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat anxiety with herbal essential oils is by blending calming and relaxing botanicals. Lavender oil's tranquil properties help calm racing thoughts, while Bergamot oil's citrus scent uplifts mood and reduces stress. Combine them with Frankincense oil, which soothes the mind and body. Apply topically to pulse points or diffuse in the air for a peaceful atmosphere, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing anxiety symptoms naturally.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to anxiety that are treated with herbal essential oils are stress, insomnia, and depression. Lavender oil is often used to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety symptoms. Bergamot oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, while Frankincense oil helps reduce inflammation and promotes emotional balance. Other herbs like chamomile, lemon balm, and ylang-ylang are also commonly used to alleviate anxiety-like symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's aroma, which is often described as peaceful and relaxing, has been shown to reduce stress and promote a sense of tranquility. The terpenes present in the oil, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, contribute to its anxiolytic effects by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting a state of relaxation. This can lead to a decrease in anxiety symptoms and an improvement in overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with anxiety by acting as a natural anxiolytic and sedative, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to anxiety relief by having a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting emotional balance and reducing anxiety symptoms.
Caryophyllene oxide: Plays a role in anxiety reduction by interacting with the body's GABA receptors, which helps to regulate and calm the nervous system, leading to a decrease in anxiety levels.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which contribute to their calming effects.
Stems: The stems contain a smaller amount of the same calming compounds as the flowers, but are still used to create a less intense version of the essential oil.
Leaves: Some producers use the leaves to create a more affordable alternative to flower-based essential oils, although they may not have the same potency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny day when the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100°c using a still with a 10:1 ratio of flowers to water.
Collect the distillate and separate it from the water using a condenser at a temperature of 40°c.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or particulate matter.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and sedative properties. The oil contains valerenic acid, a compound that interacts with the brain's GABA receptors to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties help to soothe the nervous system, leading to a sense of calm and tranquility. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate anxiety and promote a restful night's sleep, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution to their anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps to reduce anxiety by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which calms the nervous system and promotes relaxation.
Valeranone: Exerts anxiolytic effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin, contributing to a sense of calm and reduced anxiety.
Isovaleric acid: Contributes to anxiety relief by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which helps alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which is responsible for the plant's sedative and anxiolytic properties.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes also contain valerenic acid, making them a popular choice for anxiety relief essential oils.
Barks: Valerian barks contain a mixture of sesquiterpenes and valerenic acid, contributing to their anxiolytic and sedative effects in essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated with clean scissors.
Dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried roots in a copper still with a ratio of 1 part roots to 2 parts water for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or a separating funnel within 30 minutes of distillation completion.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil contains linalool and geraniol, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. It also has a balancing effect on the emotions, helping to calm the mind and body. As a result, Melissa officinalis essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, promoting a sense of calm and well-being, and supporting overall mental health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps with anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels through its sedative and calming properties.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that helps with anxiety by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting a sense of calmness and balance.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps with anxiety by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high content of melissic acid, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of linalool and other compounds that contribute to their anxiolytic properties.
Stems: The stems, like the leaves, also contain melissic acid, making them a secondary source of the essential oil for anxiety relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency when in full bloom during warm weather conditions.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature of 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a series of paper filters to remove impurities and debris.
Store the filtered melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with anxiety because its calming properties promote relaxation and soothe the mind. Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, it supports the body's natural response to stress, reducing feelings of overwhelm and unease. By balancing the nervous system and promoting a sense of calm, Passiflora incarnata essential oils can help alleviate anxiety symptoms, making it an attractive natural remedy for those seeking relief from anxiety and promoting a peaceful state of mind.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: Isovitexin helps with anxiety by acting as an anxiolytic and sedative agent, reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
Verbascoside: Verbascoside exhibits anxiolytic and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its calming effects on the nervous system, thereby reducing anxiety.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid possesses anxiolytic and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate stress and anxiety by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain high amounts of flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to their anxiolytic properties.
Flowers: They are used because they are rich in flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to have anti-anxiety effects.
Stems: They are used because they contain a significant amount of passiflorine, a flavonoid with sedative and anxiolytic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-150°f for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator funnel within 30 minutes.
Store the extracted passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 40-60°f for 24 hours.
Filter the stored essential oil using a coffee filter paper 1-2 times before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anthemis nobilis essential oils helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil, also known as chamomile essential oil, has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. Its gentle, herbal scent can help calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and relax. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate anxiety-related symptoms such as irritability and restlessness, promoting a sense of peace and tranquility. This can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Chamazulene: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce anxiety by calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation in the brain.
Caryophyllene oxide: This terpene has anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with the body's GABA receptors, leading to a calming and relaxing effect on the mind and body.
Linalool: This monoterpenoid alcohol has a sedative and anxiolytic effect, helping to reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving mood.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used for their calming and soothing properties, which help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation.
Leaves: They contain essential oils with antispasmodic and anti-anxiety properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety.
Roots: They are used for their grounding and calming effects, which can help reduce anxiety and promote a sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers of anthemis nobilis by hand in the early morning or late afternoon to avoid heat stress.
Steam distill 100-150 grams of fresh flowers in 1 liter of distilled water for 2-3 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities and water residue.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to protect from light and contamination.
Label and date the bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with anxiety because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind. The oil is derived from oats and is known for its soothing properties. It contains a compound called avenacoside, which has a sedative effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety. Regular use of Avena sativa essential oil can lead to improved sleep quality, reduced muscle tension, and a sense of calmness, making it a popular natural remedy for anxiety relief. It also supports the body's natural ability to cope with stress.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: This sesquiterpene lactone helps alleviate anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels due to its anxiolytic properties.
Avenalol: This sesquiterpene alcohol exhibits anxiolytic and sedative effects, contributing to reduced anxiety by promoting relaxation and sleep quality.
Avenasterol: This triterpene has a sedative effect, which helps alleviate anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Stems: Stems are the most commonly used part for making essential oils for anxiety due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have calming and relaxing properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used for making essential oils for anxiety because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-anxiety and stress-reducing effects.
Seeds: Seeds are used for making essential oils for anxiety due to their high content of sesquiterpenes and lignans, which have sedative and calming properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh avena sativa roots and leaves for distillation.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried avena sativa material at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separating funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils helps with anxiety because of its unique properties and benefits. This oil, also known as Australian sandalwood, has a grounding and calming effect on the mind and body. Its woody and earthy scent can promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality. By reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting a sense of calm, Santalum album essential oils can help individuals manage anxiety and promote overall well-being. It can also improve focus and concentration.
remedy_constituents: Beta-Santalol: Helps reduce anxiety by exhibiting a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and calming the mind.
Alpha-Santalol: Contributes to anxiety relief by reducing stress hormones and promoting a sense of calmness and tranquility, which helps alleviate anxiety symptoms.
Geranyl Acetate: Plays a role in anxiety management by possessing anxiolytic and sedative properties, which help to calm the nervous system and promote a sense of relaxation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of the Santalum album plant due to their high concentration of santalol, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Leaves: Leaves of the Santalum album plant are also used to make essential oils, which have been found to have a balancing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress.
Buds: Buds of the Santalum album plant are used to make essential oils, which are rich in sesquiterpenes that have a calming and grounding effect on the mind and body, helping to alleviate anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh santalum album leaves and twigs from mature plants at dawn when dew is still present on the foliage.
Steam distill the harvested material at a temperature range of 80-100 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water phase using a separatory funnel.
Store the extracted essential oil in airtight glass containers away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
Allow the essential oil to mature and stabilize for 24-48 hours before using or bottling it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with anxiety because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind. The oil contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which work together to reduce stress and anxiety levels. It also improves mood and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain, allowing for a clearer and more focused mind. This, in turn, helps individuals to manage anxiety and feel more grounded and centered, reducing feelings of overwhelm and promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps reduce anxiety by acting as a GABA agonist, promoting relaxation and calming effects on the nervous system.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Contributes to anxiety relief by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to anxiety and other mood disorders.
Ginkgolides: Exhibits anxiolytic effects by blocking the action of platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound involved in the development of anxiety-like behaviors and stress responses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba for essential oils due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have a calming effect.
Buds: Buds are also used for essential oils, as they contain a higher concentration of terpenoids than leaves, providing anxiolytic properties.
Seeds: Seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used for essential oils, which are rich in ginkgolides and bilobalide, compounds that have been shown to have a positive effect on anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 kg of ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer when they are mature and slightly yellow.
Steam distill the leaves at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to separate the essential oil from water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separator or decanter.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 4°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with anxiety because of its unique properties that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The oil contains flavonoids and naringenin, which have been shown to have anxiolytic effects by modulating neurotransmitter activity. It also has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety such as restlessness and irritability. Additionally, the oil's antidepressant properties can help to regulate mood and improve overall emotional well-being, making it a natural remedy for anxiety relief.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It acts as a serotonin reuptake inhibitor, which helps in increasing serotonin levels in the brain, thereby reducing anxiety.
N-feruloylserotonin: It has anxiolytic properties due to its ability to modulate neurotransmitter activity and reduce stress-induced anxiety.

Oligoflavonoids (e.g., rutin and quercetin): These flavonoids have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, helping to reduce anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum are the most commonly used part for making essential oils due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have a calming effect.

Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are also used for making essential oils, which contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help to reduce anxiety and stress.

Roots: The roots of Hypericum perforatum are used for making essential oils, which contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that have a sedative effect and can help to alleviate anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowering tops of hypericum perforatum by cutting them with scissors in the early morning hours.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried plant material at 100-150 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil using a paper filter to remove impurities and debris.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with anxiety because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties. These properties enable the oil to reduce stress and promote relaxation by calming the nervous system and regulating cortisol levels. Boswellia serrata essential oil also has a grounding and soothing effect, which can help individuals feel more centered and focused. As a result, it can provide relief from anxiety and promote emotional well-being, leading to a more balanced and peaceful state of mind.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-boswellic acid: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help reduce anxiety by mitigating the body's inflammatory response and promoting a sense of calmness.
Beta-boswellic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-anxiety properties, likely due to its ability to modulate the body's stress response and promote relaxation.

Furanosesquiterpenes (such as incensole acetate): Have been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors, leading to a decrease in anxiety and an increase in feelings of calmness and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in boswellic acids, which are responsible for the anti-anxiety and stress-relieving properties of the essential oil.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata contain high levels of boswellic acids, making them a primary source of the essential oil's anxiolytic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata resin by cutting the branches of the tree to collect the sap that seeps out.
Dry the collected resin in a warm and dry place for at least 7 days to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill the dried resin using a distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours to extract the oil.
Separate the extracted oil from the water and impurities using a separation funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the extracted oil through a filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and achieve a clear consistency.
